confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_64cf33b1-83cf-43ec-9dda-91ef6f922a6a read back as received

narada_alignment: This is in line with Narada telos because it increases coherence while preserving future freedom.
It prevents false readiness: fully_idle should mean no current work in admitted layers, not that onboarding is complete across unselected capabilities.
It supports inhabited evolution: capabilities are admitted through lived need and made explicit before mutation.

questions: Which execution surface is intended: Windows native, WSL-assisted, OneDrive non-git, Git-backed, PC-locus, User-locus, client-service locus?
Which capabilities should this Site admit now?
Examples: mailbox/intake, operator surface, daemon/runtime, task machinery, KB, data/ELT affinity, Git/GitHub sync, notifications, outbound effects.
For each selected capability, ask only its material choices.
Mailbox example: address, admission domains, folders, attachment policy, draft/send posture, credential binding, historical window.

proposed_layers.rule: If Operator defers a material choice, record the deferral explicitly with scope and next review trigger.

rationale: Narada setup should dearbitrarize by following the structure of the Site rather than asking ad hoc questions as they occur to the architect.
Substrate and authority locus must be decided before mutations so Windows/User/PC/client-service authority does not collapse into the caller shell or current repo clone.
Capabilities should be admitted explicitly. A structurally initialized Site should not imply mailbox/runtime/operator-surface/data capability readiness.
Deferred choices should be recorded as deferred choices, not as silent absence.
requested_execution: Document cascading onboarding in Narada product/doctrine docs.
Update client-service bootstrap/readiness docs to separate structural readiness from capability readiness.
Consider CLI support for a guided/resumable onboarding flow that emits numbered Operator choices and records deferred choices.
payload.summary: Repeated Site onboarding work suggests Narada should treat onboarding as a cascading, capability-aware process: first decide substrate/locus, then select capabilities, then configure each selected capability, then report readiness by layer. This prevents false readiness and hidden omissions such as missing mailbox posture after structural bootstrap.
payload.title: Adopt cascading onboarding for Narada Sites
